ops.title: LoginItem: reconciliation over one-shot flags, and the SMAppService seam
ops.body:
# Reconciliation, not a one-shot flag

reconcile() switches on the LIVE SMAppService status every call: .enabled → active; .requiresApproval → opens the Login Items settings pane, returns pendingApproval; .notFound / .notRegistered → attempts register(). A one-shot "have I registered?" boolean would wedge forever the first time status reads .notFound (the item was never seen), because that boolean is never set — reconciling on real status each time is the bug this design kills.

# The seam and Sendable design

LoginItemService is the seam over SMAppService because SMAppService itself cannot run under `swift test` — tests inject a fake. LoginItemAgent stores only the plistName (keeping the value Sendable); the non-Sendable SMAppService handle is re-derived per call — the handle is a thin wrapper over the name, so this is free. The agent plist lives in the app's Contents/Library/LaunchAgents.
